skill.name: project-to-obsidian
skill.description: Converts a code project into a structured Obsidian knowledge base with project overview, architecture notes, API references, module pages, quickstart guides, and Dataview indexes.
skill.summary: Generate Obsidian-ready documentation from a software project.
skill.author: Claude Code

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and weak crypto patterns, but review found these are Markdown code fences, placeholders, command examples, and file extension text rather than executable code. The confirmed risk is that the skill instructs agents to scan .env and config files, which can expose secrets in generated Obsidian documentation if values are copied without redaction.

medium_findings.title: Potential Secret Exposure From Environment File Scanning
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to scan .env and config files and later generate configuration reference notes. This is useful for documentation, but it can copy secrets, tokens, or private connection strings into Obsidian notes if the agent does not redact values.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly includes .env patterns in the scan list and documents environment variables as generated output. I did not find network exfiltration, so the risk is local disclosure through generated documentation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified As Executable Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings in README.md, SKILL.md, and templates are mostly fenced Markdown examples, slash-command examples, diagrams, or template placeholders. They are not executable scripts in this skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are documentation blocks and templates. No shell script, Ruby code, or dynamic command execution implementation was found in these files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Markdown Extension False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings appear to be triggered by text such as SKILL.md, Markdown templates, or the .md extension. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic implementation was found in the reviewed content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are metadata, headings, or placeholders. I did not find code that computes hashes or encrypts data.

low_findings.title: Home Directory Install Path Is A Normal Skill Installation Example
low_findings.description: README.md shows copying the skill into ~/.claude/skills for installation. This writes into a hidden home directory, but it is a standard local installation path and not hidden persistence code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is presented as a manual install instruction. There is no autorun hook, credential access, or stealth behavior tied to this path.

low_findings.title: Obfuscation Heuristics Are Caused By Template Syntax And Non-English Documentation
low_findings.description: The high entropy and bracket-chain findings are consistent with Chinese documentation, Markdown tables, diagrams, and template placeholders. No encoded payload, packed script, or self-decoding content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious structure is visible as normal Markdown content and templating. I did not find base64 decoding, eval behavior, or hidden binary data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Configuration Files Included In Documentation Scope
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes .env and config files in the project scan scope. Generated documentation should record variable names and purposes only, not secret values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicitly documented and connected to generated configuration references. The absence of network calls lowers the severity from high to medium.

content.user_title: Convert Projects to Obsidian Knowledge Bases
content.value_statement: Large code projects are hard to understand when architecture, APIs, and modules are spread across many files. This skill analyzes a project and creates Obsidian-ready notes with links, indexes, and structured documentation.
actual_capabilities: Scans project structure to identify languages, frameworks, entry files, modules, APIs, and configuration files.
Generates Obsidian note structures for project overview, quickstart, architecture, API documentation, and module documentation.
Creates wiki links, tags, frontmatter properties, and Dataview queries for navigation inside an Obsidian vault.
Supports full, quick, API-focused, and architecture-focused documentation modes.
Can write output to an Obsidian vault, a local folder, or a project docs directory after user confirmation.
limitations: Generated documentation depends on the agent reading and interpreting the project correctly.
Very large projects may need quick mode or a smaller target directory.
Obsidian vault writing requires compatible MCP tools or a local file output fallback.
Sensitive configuration values require manual redaction rules before notes are published or shared.

use_cases.title: Onboard a New Developer
use_cases.description: Create linked overview, quickstart, architecture, and module notes so a new teammate can understand a codebase faster.

use_cases.title: Document an API Service
use_cases.description: Extract API endpoints, request details, response notes, and related modules into an Obsidian reference set.

use_cases.title: Build a Personal Code Research Vault
use_cases.description: Turn open source or internal projects into searchable notes with Dataview indexes and cross-linked concepts.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Quick Project Overview
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner scan for a small project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use project-to-obsidian on this project in quick mode. Create a project overview, directory structure, and technology stack notes only.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Full Obsidian Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this repository into an Obsidian knowledge base. Include overview, quickstart, architecture, API documentation, module notes, and Dataview indexes.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on API Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the backend folder and generate Obsidian API documentation. Include endpoints, methods, parameters, authentication notes, responses, and related modules.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Reviewed Architecture Vault
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced documentation with sensitive data controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate architecture and module documentation for this project. Do not copy secret values from .env or config files. Document only variable names, purposes, and safe defaults.

output_examples.input: A TypeScript Express API project with routes, services, and package metadata.
output: A project overview note with language, framework, repository, dependency, and module links.
An API index with endpoint notes grouped by route area.
Module notes describing service responsibilities, core functions, dependencies, and related files.

output_examples.input: A Python FastAPI project where the user selects quick mode.
output: A compact Obsidian folder containing overview, directory structure, and technology stack notes.
Dataview queries for module and recent-change navigation.
Links to suggested follow-up documentation areas.

output_examples.input: A Rust service that needs architecture documentation.
output: Architecture notes with component summaries, data flow, external dependencies, and performance considerations.
Linked module pages for crates and key source files.
A quickstart note with prerequisites, build commands, run steps, and validation guidance.

best_practices: Confirm the output location before writing notes to a vault or project directory.
Redact secrets from .env and config files before generating configuration documentation.
Review generated notes before moving them from an AI inbox into permanent Obsidian project folders.
anti_patterns: Do not run full mode on a huge monorepo without narrowing the target directory.
Do not publish generated configuration notes that include real tokens, passwords, or private URLs.
Do not overwrite existing Obsidian notes without a review and backup process.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates Obsidian-ready project documentation, including overview, quickstart, architecture, API, and module notes.

faq.question: Does it require Obsidian MCP?
faq.answer: No. MCP enables direct vault writing, but the skill can also create local folders or project docs output.

faq.question: Can it document API endpoints?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an API mode that focuses on routes, methods, parameters, responses, and related modules.

faq.question: How should secrets be handled?
faq.answer: Do not copy secret values into generated notes. Document variable names and purposes, then redact sensitive values.

faq.question: Which project types are supported?
faq.answer: It describes support for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, and common web frameworks.

faq.question: Is the generated vault ready for long-term use?
faq.answer: It is a strong starting point. Review links, descriptions, and sensitive configuration notes before making it permanent.
